missing_info: Latest interface status (including admin_state, oper_state, last_change) for R2 Serial0/1/1 and R3 Serial0/1/1 — e.g., output of 'show interface Serial0/1/1' or equivalent get_interface_status with timestamps.

Syslog / logging entries that show the state-change events for Serial0/1/1 on R2 and R3 (search for messages containing 'Serial0/1/1' and include timestamps) to correlate when 'lower-layer-down' or 'administratively down' occurred.
Running-config for R3 interface Serial0/1/1 (show running-config interface Serial0/1/1) to confirm whether 'shutdown' is configured or was applied recently.

plan_history.intent_description: R2 Serial0/1/1 is oper 'lower-layer-down' with admin up (likely physical/link-layer fault). R3 Serial0/1/1 is administratively down. Impact: possible loss of inter-router connectivity. Desired outcome: bring R3 Serial0/1/1 admin up and either restore R2 Serial0/1/1 to oper up or mark it admin down to reflect an intended outage.
plan_history.problem: R2 Serial0/1/1 operational 'lower-layer-down' while admin is up; R3 Serial0/1/1 is administratively down — inter-router link impaired.
plan_history.fix_summary: Bring R3 Serial0/1/1 admin up. Mark R2 Serial0/1/1 admin down to make state consistent until physical/link issue is resolved.

remaining_issues: Physical/link problem remains: R3 Serial0/1/1 oper_state is still "lower-layer-down", indicating the underlying link is not up and the adjacency is not restored.
Root cause not yet identified — further troubleshooting required (cabling, clocking, CSU/DSU, or remote side interface state).
